stations.name: Murarrie railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Walk time depends on which Murarrie pocket you live in. The northern residential streets are within a 10 to 15 minute walk; properties further south are a longer walk or a short drive.

stations.name: Cannon Hill railway station
stations.walk_minutes_note: Cannon Hill station is walkable from a number of Murarrie streets and is the closest station for parts of the western residential pocket.

trains.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 18 to 22 minutes by train to Central station off-peak. Verify current schedules on Translink.

buses.note: Bus routes through Murarrie change with Translink service updates. Search Translink with your address to see which routes serve your street, current frequencies, and live arrival times.

driving.motorway_access: Gateway Motorway via the Wynnum Road and Lytton Road interchanges
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_offpeak_note: Approximately 12 to 15 minutes to Brisbane CBD off-peak via Wynnum Road or the Story Bridge.
driving.approx_cbd_minutes_peak_note: Peak commute can extend to 25 to 35 minutes depending on route and conditions.
driving.airport_access_note: Direct Gateway Motorway access makes Brisbane Airport approximately 10 to 12 minutes off-peak.

connections: Bulimba Creek bikeway connecting north toward the Brisbane River
Riverside paths accessible via Cannon Hill and Hawthorne for longer rides
bike_walk.note: Active transport connections from Murarrie to Bulimba and Hawthorne are practical for confident riders. Casual cyclists should plan routes via Translink's cycle map.

primary_state.name: Murarrie State School
primary_state.note: State primary school within the suburb. Catchment boundary should be verified via the QGSO catchment lookup before any specific street is implied to be in-catchment.

secondary_state.name: Catchment varies by street
secondary_state.note: Murarrie residential streets fall within different state high school catchments depending on the precise address. Use the QGSO lookup to confirm for any given property.

parks: Bulimba Creek parkland and walking tracks
lifestyle.shops_dining_note: Daily-life retail and cafes are a short drive at Bulimba Plaza, the Hawthorne Garage precinct, and Cannon Hill K-Mart Plaza. The Bulimba and Hawthorne village strips are within five to seven minutes by car for restaurants, bars, and weekend dining.
lifestyle.river_access: Brisbane River frontage along the suburb's northern boundary, with public access via Murarrie Recreation Reserve and adjoining parkland.

pockets.name: Northern residential pocket
pockets.character: Conventional residential streets around Hargreaves Street and the area north of Murarrie Road. Fully residential, no industrial adjacency. The closest pocket to Cannon Hill station and Bulimba village amenity.

pockets.name: Metroplex-adjacent
pockets.character: Newer builds and contemporary homes with proximity to the Metroplex on Gateway business precinct. Convenient for residents working at the precinct or using the Gateway Motorway daily.

pockets.name: Creek Road corridor
pockets.character: Residential streets adjacent to the Creek Road and Tingalpa Creek light industrial corridor. The market prices the industrial adjacency accurately. Buyers who prioritise entry price over absolute amenity find genuine value here.

pockets.name: Riverside
pockets.character: Streets closest to the Brisbane River frontage and Murarrie Recreation Reserve. Open-space access and outlook are the defining features.

buyer_profile.note: Buyers who started their search in Cannon Hill, Morningside, or Hawthorne and redirected eastward when the entry point pushed beyond their budget.

Sydney and Melbourne buyers relocating to Brisbane for work. The combination of airport proximity, motorway access, and inner-east lifestyle within a more accessible price point lands well with this group.
Local first home buyers entering the inner east at an accessible price point relative to the surrounding suburbs.

Investors who understand Brisbane's yield narrative and are buying on the numbers. Murarrie's rental returns can outperform more established neighbouring suburbs because entry prices are lower while rental demand remains solid.
